codeReview.summary: SqliteMemoryStore implementation exists in lib.rs. Feature was already implemented prior to this worker session. The implementation passes tests (374 tests pass, clippy clean) and core CRUD/search/filter operations work. However, a blocking issue remains: search() does NOT filter by source_episode_id even though MemoryQuery has this field.

issues.description: search() method does not filter by source_episode_id even though MemoryQuery struct has this field. The search() implementation filters by repository, is_stale, query_text, block_types, labels, language, and task_type, but source_episode_id filter is missing. This means provenance-based search filtering is only available via get_by_provenance() method, not through general search().

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specified skill 'rust-worker' which does not exist. AGENTS.md lists 'rust' skill (not 'rust-worker'). Worker correctly recognized skill doesn't exist and adapted. This is a documentation mismatch - features.json should use 'rust' not 'rust-worker'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json skillName='rust-worker' vs AGENTS.md skill table showing 'rust' skill. Transcript shows: Skill "rust-worker" not found

sharedStateObservations.observation: CommitId in handoff (9229e2f) does not correspond to memory-sqlite-store implementation. Commit 9229e2f is 'feat(validation): user-testing synthesis for state milestone'. The memory-sqlite-store feature was implemented in an earlier commit not recorded in the handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 9229e2f --stat shows validation/state/user-testing/synthesis.json changed, not any memory code.

summary: Feature memory-sqlite-store FAILS due to blocking issue: search() doesn't filter by source_episode_id. Core CRUD operations work correctly, repository scoping is properly implemented for get_by_type/get_by_label/get_by_provenance, and all 374 tests pass. The provenance fields (source_episode_id, evidence, provenance_context) ARE stored correctly via store(). However, the search() method ignores the source_episode_id field in MemoryQuery, preventing provenance-based filtering in general search operations.
